Land Rover Range Rover P510e Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Range Rover P510e engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Range Rover P510e (PT306 AJ20P6) engine typically costs £5,500–£8,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 at independent specialist rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Range Rover P510e engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The PT306 straight-six Ingenium petrol engine is robust and proven — it has no known catastrophic mechanical failures. However, the P510e is a complex PHEV with a 38.2kWh battery, 105kW electric motor, and AdBlue emissions system. Reliability depends on maintaining the PHEV system as much as the engine itself.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Range Rover P510e?

acceptedAnswer.text: PHEV battery and charging system issues (software glitches, timed charging failures), AdBlue/SCR system faults (crystallisation and sensor failures common on PHEVs), and cooling system complexity (three separate cooling circuits). All are manageable with proper maintenance and early detection.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover P510e engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Against a well-maintained L460 Range Rover (values £60,000–£120,000+), a £6,000–£8,000 reconditioned engine is 5–15% of the vehicle's value — worth it. The Land Rover scrap threshold of 60% means replacement is almost always financially viable on a P510e, given the L460's strong residual values.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Range Rover P510e engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code PT306 (AJ20P6) is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The P510e badge was offered from the L460's launch in 2022 until the 2024 model year update (replaced by P550e). If your L460 is a 2022–2023 PHEV with the highest-output powertrain, it is the P510e. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the PHEV battery, or does it require replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: PHEV battery issues are often software-related and repairable without replacing the battery. Battery management system (BMS) recalibration and software updates cost £500–£2,000. However, if the battery cells have degraded significantly (range drop of 30%+), cell replacement or full battery replacement may be required — £5,000–£10,000+. Regular use and avoiding deep discharges are the best prevention.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Range Rover P510e Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current Range Rover L460 P510e PT306 PHEV era. This badge was offered 2022–2023, so no older-generation variance applies.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a P510e engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The PHEV system's high-voltage components require specialist handling. [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover P510e engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: PHEV Battery & Charging System Issues
problems.symptoms: Reduced electric range, failure to charge overnight, timed charging not working, battery warning light, charging port errors, reduced power in hybrid mode.
problems.repairCost: £500–£2,000 (software updates, BMS recalibration, or component replacement) [PLACEHOLDER]

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if charging fails or range drops significantly

problems.recommendation: The P510e's 38.2kWh lithium-ion battery is the heart of the PHEV system. While generally reliable, some owners have reported timed charging failures and software glitches. The battery management system (BMS) requires regular software updates. If the vehicle fails to charge or the electric range drops dramatically, get the high-voltage system diagnosed immediately. Battery replacement is rare but extremely expensive — maintaining the battery through regular use and avoiding deep discharges is essential.

problems.title: AdBlue / SCR System Failures (PHEV-Specific Complexity)
problems.symptoms: Emissions warning light, AdBlue countdown timer, reduced power, MOT failure, "Emissions System Malfunction" warning.
problems.repairCost: £400–£1,500 (sensor replacement, AdBlue injector cleaning, or crystallisation removal) [PLACEHOLDER]

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if AdBlue countdown appears

problems.recommendation: The P510e's emissions system includes an AdBlue (SCR) system to meet Euro 6d standards. In PHEV operation, the engine runs less frequently, which can lead to AdBlue crystallisation in the injector or exhaust system. This is a known issue across modern JLR diesels and petrol PHEVs. If the countdown timer starts or you get emissions warnings, do not ignore it — once the timer reaches zero, the engine will not restart. Regular use of the combustion engine and AdBlue system maintenance are the best prevention.

problems.title: Cooling System Complexity (Engine + Battery + Electric Motor)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss, temperature warnings, reduced power in hot weather, occasional overheating.
problems.repairCost: £300–£1,200 (hose replacement, coolant pump, or radiator repair) [PLACEHOLDER]
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (unless overheating leads to engine damage)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; monitor coolant levels regularly

problems.recommendation: The P510e has three separate cooling systems: one for the combustion engine, one for the electric motor/inverter, and one for the high-voltage battery. This complexity means more potential failure points — coolant hoses, pumps, and radiators all need monitoring. Some early L460 P510es have had cooling system component replacements under warranty. If you notice coolant loss or temperature warnings, have the system pressure-tested immediately.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover P510e — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Software updates and BMS recalibration are straightforward
Extremely rare; assess against vehicle value if it occurs
The straight-six Ingenium is robust; no known catastrophic failure pattern

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Range Rover P510e? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with PHEV system checks recorded
* Battery health report available (shows capacity and cell balance)
* AdBlue system recently serviced with no warnings
* Cooling system inspected and no coolant loss history
* Vehicle has been regularly used (not left standing — batteries degrade when unused)
avoidIf: * Charging issues reported with no diagnosis — could be expensive battery or BMS fault
* AdBlue countdown active with no proof of repair — costly emissions system work ahead
* Battery range significantly below expectations — indicates degradation or BMS issues
* Any high-voltage warning lights — specialist diagnostic equipment required

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Range Rover P510e is a technological tour de force — a 510PS plug-in hybrid that delivers supercar performance with 70 miles of electric-only range. The straight-six Ingenium petrol engine is robust and proven. The complexity lies in the PHEV system: battery, charging, and emissions hardware. Buy on full service history, ensure the battery is healthy, and maintain the AdBlue system. This is a £100,000+ luxury SUV — the running costs reflect that.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover P510e engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover P510e (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Battery Health — Request a battery health report; range should be close to the 70-mile WLTP claim
* Charging Test — Does it charge correctly on both AC and DC chargers? Any timed charging issues?
* AdBlue System — Any warnings or countdowns? Check service history for AdBlue work
* Coolant Level — Check cold and hot; any loss indicates cooling system issues (engine, battery, or motor)
* Service History — Regular services with PHEV system checks are essential
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Emissions, battery, or cooling codes are most common
* Electric Range — Does the displayed range match expected? Significant drops indicate battery degradation
* MOT History — Check for emissions failures (AdBlue/SCR-related failures are common on PHEVs)

However, the P510e is a complex PHEV with a 38.2kWh battery, 105kW electric motor, and AdBlue emissions system. Reliability depends on maintaining the PHEV system as much as the engine itself.
items.value: 3. What are the common problems with the Range Rover P510e?

The P510e badge was offered from the L460's launch in 2022 until the 2024 model year update (replaced by P550e). If your L460 is a 2022–2023 PHEV with the highest-output powertrain, it is the P510e. items.label: PHEV battery issues are often software-related and repairable without replacing the battery. Battery management system (BMS) recalibration and software updates cost £500–£2,000. Regular use and avoiding deep discharges are the best prevention.
